_comment: step 5 live-spike evidence. Part A = success (real reviewer headless roundtrip). Part B history = REFUTATION -> REMEDIATION -> RE-PROOF: (1) the first run refuted the spec 5-4 premise ('read-only/tier profile makes vsp refuse writes') because the then-locked binary (v2.38.1-91-g0b03ef2) deploy path consumed neither SAP_READ_ONLY nor SAP_TIER for the pilot write shape ($TMP deploy, no transport) - recorded honestly as ok:false; no SAP write was ever performed. (2) Remediation: vsp-custom commit 5a8bedb (build v2.38.1-94-g5a8bedb) added a client-side, pre-network write-profile gate to write-capable CLI subcommands (enforceWriteProfile via getWriteClient); lock adapters/vsp/vsp.lock.json re-verified/updated to v2.38.1-94 with write_profile_gate.block_live=PENDING. (3) Re-proof (this session, AC-10): the new binary now refuses the exact pilot write shape client-side with the new markers; measured with SAP write실수행 0. part_b is now ok:true; emergency procedure did NOT fire.

part_a.notes: review-gate.mjs full pipeline exit 0 (PASS), reviewer spawn duration 49262ms recorded in state.json pass_records. Command array confirmed by measurement and recorded in scripts/review-gate/config.json reviewer.command (claude -p <prompt> --append-system-prompt <json-only guard> --model opus --output-format text --strict-mcp-config --disallowedTools Write Edit NotebookEdit Bash WebFetch WebSearch Task). Calibration iteration 1 produced analysis prose before the JSON (would classify MALFORMED); prompt hardened to raw-JSON-only, iteration 2 returned a pure 123-byte JSON verdict (56188ms, saved as verdict_file). Clean allowlist-env spawn verified: reviewer received only the 9 allowlisted vars, no SAP_* connection vars.

part_b.refusal_marker: blocked: SAP_READ_ONLY=true — write operations are disabled for this profile (unset SAP_READ_ONLY or set it to false to enable writes)
part_b.refusal_marker_tier: blocked: SAP_TIER=QA — write operations are only allowed on the dev tier (unset SAP_TIER or set SAP_TIER=dev to enable writes)
part_b.attempted_write_shape: vsp deploy zsah3_spike.prog.abap $TMP (no --transport) — the exact pilot write shape the OLD binary did NOT gate (see _comment refutation); this is the shape now refused client-side.
part_b.binary: vsp version v2.38.1-94-g5a8bedb (commit: 5a8bedb, built: 2026-07-18T02:53:23Z); binary sha256 matches adapters/vsp/vsp.lock.json verified_commit 5a8bedb866c60f54ef76a752e86b167dbabb6e0a. Resolves lock write_profile_gate.verified.block_live (was PENDING(Part B)).
part_b.gate_mechanism: New client-side write gate in vsp-custom 5a8bedb: write-capable subcommands (deploy/copy/execute/install/source write/edit) build their ADT client via getWriteClient = enforceWriteProfile()+getClient (cmd/vsp/cli.go:203-222). enforceWriteProfile() runs BEFORE any network call: viper.GetBool('READ_ONLY') (SAP_READ_ONLY, bound via package init() SetEnvPrefix('SAP')+AutomaticEnv at cmd/vsp/main.go:206-208) blocks all writes when truthy; SAP_TIER != 'dev' (case-insensitive, non-empty) blocks writes. runDeploy calls getWriteClient (devops.go:2898) before DeployFromFile network call (devops.go:2908). Read-only commands (system info, source read) call getClient directly and are never gated — reads unaffected.

probes.probe1_readonly_copy: env from profile copy with SAP_TIER=QA + SAP_READ_ONLY=true. pre-read 'vsp source read PROG ZSAH3_SPIKE' -> exit 1, ADT status 404 (target absent). 'vsp deploy zsah3_spike.prog.abap $TMP' -> exit 1, refusal_marker above; measured gated=yes, created=no, dialed=no (client-side refusal BEFORE any network dial). post-read 'vsp source read PROG ZSAH3_SPIKE' -> exit 1, status 404 again (nothing created).
probes.probe2_tier_only_copy: env from profile copy with SAP_TIER=QA and NO SAP_READ_ONLY (isolates the tier gate). 'vsp deploy zsah3_spike.prog.abap $TMP' -> exit 1, refusal_marker_tier above; gated=yes, created=no, dialed=no. Directly satisfies AC-10 'tier gate refusal'.
probes.probe3_read_separation: same read-only copy env -> 'vsp system info' -> exit 0. Connection is normal, so the deploy refusals are the write gate, not a connection failure.

part_b.no_write_performed: Emergency procedure did NOT fire. Both deploy probes measured created=no and dialed=no — the client-side gate refused before the network dial, so no SAP write was ever executed on any tier. Pre/post source-read 404 pair confirms ZSAH3_SPIKE was never created. R-003 honored: only the tier LABEL on the profile copy was changed to QA; the actual connection target stayed the DEV box (IDES-DEV), and system info against it succeeded.
part_b.method: scripts/vsp-env.ps1 NOT used (parallel task editing it). The original IDES-DEV profile was copied to an OS temp dir and parsed directly into a scoped process env (SAP_USERNAME mapped to SAP_USER per README flag table; SAP_INSECURE=true for the approved IDES-DEV self-signed cert). On this machine the profile credential is plaintext, so no keychain deref was needed (the vsp-env.ps1 CredMan/CredRead path is the fallback for keychain: references). SAP_TIER was set to QA and SAP_READ_ONLY=true added on the copy only; original profile untouched.
part_b.env_copy: Profile copies at an OS temp dir (git-bash mktemp -d under the Windows temp path; two variants: sap.env = SAP_TIER=QA+SAP_READ_ONLY=true, sap-tier.env = SAP_TIER=QA only). Original profile untouched; both copies deleted at step end (path recorded only, contents not recorded — R-005).
part_b.refuted_then_reproved: The original refutation (spec 5-4 premise did not hold for the OLD binary's pilot write shape) was VALID and drove the remediation (5a8bedb gate). This re-proof does not erase it: the gate the spec relies on now exists in the vsp CLI deploy path (SAP_READ_ONLY/SAP_TIER, client-side, pre-network) and refuses the pilot write shape as designed.
